description: This pipeline performs a complex image processing workflow, starting with segmentation and probability calculations, followed by morphological operations, thresholding, and hole filling to refine the image features. The processed images are then converted into region of interest (ROI) lists and further analyzed to extract statistical measurements. The pipeline also applies smoothing filters and watershed algorithms to enhance the image quality and separate touching objects. Overall, it aims to provide a detailed analysis of image structures through a series of interconnected processing steps.

dbb03680-6040-4d2b-95e7-334fb170a797.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
dbb03680-6040-4d2b-95e7-334fb170a797.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

237b7468-0fcd-4e23-ab20-44a887980d7d.name: ij1-threshold-auto2d
237b7468-0fcd-4e23-ab20-44a887980d7d.description:
Applies an auto-thresholding algorithm. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

9981ae91-45ca-4b9e-aaea-0d1609f59799.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
9981ae91-45ca-4b9e-aaea-0d1609f59799.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

7828a915-4129-4648-bfae-7515439a3387.name: cellpose-segmentation-inference-3.x
7828a915-4129-4648-bfae-7515439a3387.description:
Runs Cellpose on the input image with the given model(s). This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged

200c5809-536c-408c-82e2-136ba98f7b53.name: ij1-morph-binary-fillholes2d
200c5809-536c-408c-82e2-136ba98f7b53.description: Applies a morphological hole filling operation to binary images.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

f584372b-e285-4ee9-aa7e-e61dc0817c62.name: ij1-analyze-find-particles2d
f584372b-e285-4ee9-aa7e-e61dc0817c62.description: Converts mask images into ROI by applying a connected components algorithm and generates measurements. Please note that this algorithm will always trace the external object boundaries and convert them into polygonal ROIs. This means that holes will be closed. This node requires a thresholded image as input and will extract measurements from the thresholded image. If you already have ROI available and want measurements, use 'Extract ROI statistics'.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the results are generated for each 2D slice.

cd5f30ac-21e4-47a7-a620-3cbbfc922dbe.name: ij1-morph-operation2d
cd5f30ac-21e4-47a7-a620-3cbbfc922dbe.description:
Applies a morphological operation to greyscale images. Following operations are included: Erosion, Dilation, Opening, Closing, White Top Hat, Black Top Hat, Gradient, Laplacian, Internal Gradient, and External Gradient. More information (including examples) can be found at https://imagej.net/MorphoLibJ.html
If a multi-channel image is provided, the operation is applied to each channel. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

637b18d4-8d3f-4fed-a393-526812f4eb3e.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
637b18d4-8d3f-4fed-a393-526812f4eb3e.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

2b81cc60-1a14-4ef9-8c7e-188704cde5d0.name: ij1-blur-gaussian2d
2b81cc60-1a14-4ef9-8c7e-188704cde5d0.description: Applies convolution with a Gaussian function for smoothing. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

02e70774-4ccb-4788-b941-eb56b23ab77b.name: ij1-binary-dtwatershed2d
02e70774-4ccb-4788-b941-eb56b23ab77b.description: Applies an euclidean distance transform on binary images. Then applies a watershed algorithm.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.
